subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The exploration follows a clear narrative structure (introduction, theory, methods, data, evaluation), aiding reader comprehension.
A detailed table of contents and numbered headings provide a logical roadmap through the report.
Content remains mostly relevant with minimal digressions, ensuring the reader stays focused on the mathematical argument.
weaknesses: Transitions between sections (e.g. from integral derivation to averageâvalue formula) are occasionally abrupt and could be signposted more clearly.
Explanations are sometimes verbose or repetitive, reducing overall conciseness.
References to figures and equations lack explicit pointers in the text, hindering seamless coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed coherence at band 3 (logical development maintained with minor gaps), organization at band 4 (exceptionally well structured), and conciseness at band 3 (mostly relevant but verbose). Balancing these, a holistic score of 3 reflects strong overall presentation with room for tighter integration and brevity.

strengths: Mathematical notation (Ï, Î¸, k, P(Â·)) and equation numbering are used consistently in most places.
Various representations (tables, line graphs, Venn diagrams, sketches, Desmos screenshot) effectively support the analysis.
Logical progression from theory to linearization to regression is generally clear and guides the reader through the mathematics.
weaknesses: Notation inconsistencies (mixing uppercase Î and lowercase Î¸) and occasional typographic slips reduce precision.
Several figures (e.g. Figure 1, Figure 3) lack axis labels and units, diminishing clarity of representations.
Some algebraic steps are compressed or omitted (e.g. setâtheoretic justifications, intermediate integration steps), causing logical leaps.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands sit in band 3 (âmostly consistentâ notation, âgoodâ use of representations, âgoodâ logical clarity). While the student effectively communicates the mathematics, recurring minor errors and omissions prevent a band 4 or 4/4/4 overall. A holistic score of 3 accurately reflects this level.

strengths: Adaptation of Buffonâs needle experiment to a tiled kitchen floor shows genuine independent thinking.
The personal narrative (YouTube inspiration, bespoke tossing procedure) conveys a clear personal approach.
Development of three distinct methods and discussion of improvements demonstrates solid creativity and initiative.
weaknesses: Reasoning follows wellâknown derivations closely, limiting evidence of truly outstanding originality.
Experimental design choices (e.g. length range 5â23 cm) are not fully justified, reducing the depth of personal insight.
Narrative style remains relatively conventional rather than highly innovative in presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments for independent thinking, personal approach, and creativity all fell into band 2 (âsignificant evidenceâ, âclear evidenceâ, âgood initiativeâ). There is noteworthy personal engagement, but not at the exceptional band 3 levelâthus a score of 2 is appropriate.

strengths: The student provides meaningful reflection on why each method overâ or underestimates Ï and identifies sources of error.
Analysis links deviations in Ï to physical causes (grooves, rolling), showing thoughtful critical analysis.
Clear evaluation of outcomes with percentage error calculations and proposed methodological improvements.
weaknesses: Quantification of uncertainties is minimal and treated as negligible without supporting data.
Reflection lacks deeper statistical analysis (e.g. confidence intervals) and comprehensive critique.
Future implications are suggested but not explored in depth to meet the highest reflection standard.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three reflection strandsâdepth of reflection, critical analysis, and evaluationâwere assessed at band 2 (âmeaningful reflectionâ, âthoughtful analysisâ, âclear evaluationâ). This supports a holistic score of 2 rather than the highest band of 3.

strengths: Use of integral calculus, probability theory, algebraic manipulation, and linear regression is purposeful and clearly tied to the research question.
Mathematical techniques are fully appropriate for SL level, demonstrating strong command of requisite methods.
Derivations and models cover several interconnected ideas, showing comprehensive application of relevant mathematics.
weaknesses: Minor algebraic errors (misâcopied coefficients, inconsistent regression values m=0.723 vs 0.712) affect accuracy.
Uncertainty propagation is not performed, and some differential/integration steps lack explicit notation (e.g. missing dÎ¸).
Justification for selecting positive roots and certain simplifications is not always clearly explained.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments awarded band 4 for relevance and level appropriateness and band 3 for understanding/accuracy. This profile aligns with a level 5 out of 6 in the SL Use of Mathematics criterion: mathematics is used effectively and at the correct level with minor errors preventing full band 6 performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany each algebraic manipulation and graph; the progression from theory to linearisation to regression is generally clear.  Occasional logical leaps (e.g., leap from integral result to average) reduce absolute clarity, but structure remains good overall.

Derivations show good conceptual grasp, but there are algebra slips (e.g., mis-copied coefficient in method 2), an incorrect gradient substituted (0.712 vs 0.723), and no uncertainty propagation.  Understanding is good with minor errors, fitting band 3 rather than 4.

The student furnishes tables, line graphs, Venn diagram, geometric sketches, and a Desmos screenshot, all tied directly to the argument.  While effective, axis labels and units are sometimes missing or small images unreadable, so representation is good but not âhighly effectiveâ.

The exploration follows a clear narrative â introduction, theory, methods, data, evaluation â so the reader can follow the development without major gaps. The derivations for each method build on previous results in a logical order.  Minor jumps (e.g., the abrupt move from the integral to the average-value formula) and occasional repetition prevent it from reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor, but overall coherence is maintained.

Introducing three comparative methods, linearising quadratics, and discussing improvements reveal creativity and initiative, yet the mathematics and experiment stay within familiar territory; creativity is good but not exceptional.

Analysis links deviation of Ï to over/under-representation of probabilities and to physical causes (grooves, rolling).  This is a thoughtful examination, yet lacks the depth and systematic critique required for the highest band.

Percentage error calculations and suggested modifications evidence a clear evaluation of outcomes and implications.  Discussion is solid but not comprehensive enough (no statistical confidence intervals, limited exploration of alternative models) for band 3.

The student reflects on why each method over- or under-estimated Ï, discusses proportional reasoning, and lists sources of error and future changes.  Reflection is meaningful but not deeply critical throughout, fitting the âmeaningful reflectionâ descriptor.

Mathematical symbols (Ï, Î¸, k, P( )) and equation numbering are used throughout and generally correct.  A few inconsistent notations (capital Î, misplaced Ï/2) and typographic slips stop it from being fully âconsistent and accurateâ, placing it in the âmostly consistentâ band.

The choice of kitchen tiles, self-devised tossing procedure, and personal reflection on YouTube inspiration show a clear personal approach.  However, the narrative remains relatively conventional in style, not âhighly personal and innovative.â

The write-up stays on task and irrelevant digressions are minimal; however, explanations are often verbose (long narrative paragraphs restating earlier points, repeated graph descriptions) so conciseness is not optimal.  Content is mostly relevant, earning the âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ level.

Integral calculus, probability theory, algebraic manipulation, linear regression and error measures are all relevant and applied purposefully.  The mathematics covers several connected ideas effectively, but the scope is not wide or sophisticated enough for the top two bands.

Designing three different probability-based methods on a tiled floor and adapting Buffon to two dimensions demonstrates noticeable independent thinking beyond textbook replication, but the reasoning largely follows known derivations; thus evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstanding.â

A detailed table of contents, numbered headings, separate data/analysis tables and clear appendix indicate deliberate structuring.  Introduction-background-methods-evaluation-conclusion format mirrors a scientific report and is easy to navigate, fulfilling the âexceptionally well organisedâ standard.

The calculus and probability used are solidly within SL level, neither below nor significantly above.  Techniques are appropriately chosen and generally carried out correctly, meeting âfully commensurate with course level.â

annotations.comment: The studentâs introduction effectively demonstrates personal engagement by highlighting the adaptation of Buffonâs needle experiment through a unique observation of tiled floors, reflecting genuine curiosity and initiative.
The background section would benefit from clearer linkage between text and figures; explicitly reference figure numbers in the narrative to improve coherence.
Figure 1âs image lacks axis labels and a clear legend, reducing its effectiveness as a multiple representation of the concept.
In the variable table, units are inconsistently formatted and the table extends over several pages; consider splitting or resizing to enhance readability.
The transition into Buffonâs experiment narrative would be clearer if the equation references were numbered and explicitly mentioned in the text.
Equation 3 is inserted without a direct pointer in the preceding sentence; explicitly refer to it when introducing the probability expression.
The notation uses both lowercase Î¸ and uppercase Î inconsistently (e.g., in Equation 6); unify to lowercase $$\theta$$ for precision.
The formula for average value (Equation 12) displays a summation over itself, which is confusing; rewrite as $$\overline{g(\theta)}=\frac{1}{\pi/2}\int_{0}^{\pi/2}g(\theta)\,d\theta$$.
Figure 3âs caption is clear, but the axes in the graph lack labels and units; include these to improve mathematical communication.
The conclusion summarizes results effectively but could explicitly restate the research question and final $$\pi$$ values to reinforce coherence.
In Equation 5 on page 7, the step from the integral to \(k(-\cos(\frac{\pi}{2})+\cos 0)\) omits the differential; explicitly include \(d\theta\) in each step for rigor.
Separate the integration and evaluation into distinct lines to avoid compressing multiple steps into one expression.
Equation 1 on page 8 is formatted in two blocks but shows an extraneous â=\frac{\pi}{2}â in the margin; remove or clarify that stray text.
The assumptions section is extensive but could be more concise; focus on the key assumptions $$L\le D$$ and random placement only.
The experimental design describes a randomization process but does not justify the chosen length range; explain why 5â23 cm was selected.
In list item 1, clarify how lengths were ârandomly assignedâ and ensure the process avoids subconscious bias.
The derivation of Equation 4 omits explanation of the set-theoretic step; explicitly state why $$P(C=0)=1-(P(c_x)+P(c_y)-P(c_x\cap c_y)).$$
Show the intermediate factoring step between $$y^2-2y+1$$ and $$(y-1)^2$$ for clarity in Equation 8.
When isolating $$\pi$$ in Equation 14, clarify choice of the positive or negative root and its impact on the linearization.
Table 1 raw data lacks explicit mention of the total number of tosses in the header; include ân=50â to avoid ambiguity.
The description of linearization in the text is verbose and could be streamlined with a clear bullet-point summary of axes transformations.
The regression statement reports m=0.723 but substitutes 0.712 in the calculation; verify consistent values to ensure accurate $$\pi$$ estimation.
The proportionality reasoning lists multiple direct and inverse relations but contains typographical errors in symbols; proofread for clarity.
Equation 5 derivation omits units and a brief justification for the quadratic formula application; include these to strengthen the argument.
Clarify in the text why only the positive root is chosen for $$\sqrt{1-P(C=1)}$$, even though the magnitude remains the same.
The discussion on uncertainties states they are negligible without quantifying them; deeper critical reflection on measurement error is needed.
The changes and extension section insightfully proposes using a flat whiteboard and standardized launch deviceâexcellent critical reflection on methodological improvements.
